mnemonics.title: Cache Write Policies
mnemonics.content: W T B - Write Through is Bad (slower), Write Back is Best (faster)

mnemonics.title: Locality Types
mnemonics.content: T-SN: Temporal - Same item again, Spatial - Nearby items next

mnemonics.title: Cache Mapping
mnemonics.content: D-F-S: Direct - Deterministic single line, Fully - Flexible anywhere, Set - Compromise of both

mnemonics.title: Miss Types
mnemonics.content: C-C-C: Compulsory (first access), Conflict (too many map to same), Capacity (cache too small)

mnemonics.title: Cache Levels
mnemonics.content: L1-L2-L3: Larger the number, Slower the speed, Greater the size

acronyms.usage: Replacement policy that evicts the least recently accessed block
Replacement policy that evicts the oldest block in cache
Replacement policy that evicts the least frequently accessed block
The processor that cache memory serves
Main memory that cache sits between CPU and
Cache for virtual to physical address translations

visualTips.visualization: Picture a hallway with numbered lockers - each student (memory block) has exactly ONE assigned locker (cache line)
Imagine a free-for-all coat check - any coat can go to any empty hanger, but finding yours requires checking all
Think of hotel floors - each floor (set) has multiple rooms (ways), you can stay on any room on your assigned floor
Library desk = cache. If book is at desk = hit (fast). If not = miss (need to search stacks = slow)
Drafting a document - changes saved locally first, printed/committed to main memory only when needed

analogies.analogy: Like a chef's cutting board next to the stove - ingredients accessed frequently are kept close for speed, less-used items in the pantry (main memory)
The coffee mug on your desk - you keep using it repeatedly because it's right there
Shopping at a warehouse store - once you're there, you buy multiple items since you're already at the location
Like going to a library to find a book that's checked out - you made the trip but didn't get what you needed, costing time
Work desk (L1) → filing cabinet (L2) → warehouse storage (L3/main) - faster access for frequently used items
